dataset_scope: NASA PCoE FY08Q4 single-cell capacity traces evaluated through the current dsfb-battery Stage II capacity-only workflow.

entries.title: Sustained outward capacity fade
entries.regime_scope: NASA PCoE FY08Q4 single-cell capacity-only traces evaluated with the crate's default Stage II configuration.
admissibility_assumptions: Capacity is the only measured channel in this workflow.
Residual, drift, and slew are derived from the healthy-window envelope in the current crate.

pattern.residual_behavior: Residual becomes persistently negative and reaches Boundary or Violation relative to the admissibility envelope.
pattern.drift_behavior: Outward drift remains negative across multiple cycles.
pattern.slew_behavior: No unique acceleration requirement; slew may remain near zero or fluctuate while the fade structure persists.
pattern.envelope_relation: Boundary approach followed by envelope exit or stable non-admissible occupancy.

interpretation.operational_meaning: The capacity trajectory is structurally diverging from the healthy envelope ahead of the 85% threshold baseline.
candidate_mechanisms: General capacity fade processes consistent with monotone structural divergence

ambiguity_notes: This entry does not identify a unique physical mechanism.
The same structural pattern can arise under multiple degradation causes or observer biases.
exclusion_conditions: Do not treat this as validated outside the NASA PCoE capacity-only workflow.
Do not infer chemistry transfer from this entry.

supporting_instances.notes: First boundary and first violation precede the 85% threshold baseline under the default Stage II run.
Boundary onset appears well before threshold crossing, with later violation under the same configuration.
Clean escalation from boundary to violation appears before the threshold baseline.
The first non-admissible cycles and first violation both precede threshold crossing.

entries.transfer_scope: Single-cell NASA PCoE capacity-only traces evaluated with the current crate configuration.
known_failures: The bank does not distinguish among physical fade mechanisms.

notes: Derived from repeated cell evaluation using the crate's default PipelineConfig.
Evidence reflects the currently available capacity-only workflow.

entries.title: Boundary reentry before stable violation
entries.regime_scope: NASA PCoE FY08Q4 single-cell capacity-only traces under the current DSFB grammar.
admissibility_assumptions: Boundary state reflects envelope approach or persistence-gated outward drift.
Return to Admissible is interpreted structurally rather than as proof of recovery.

pattern.residual_behavior: Residual approaches the boundary region and then returns inside the admissibility envelope before the first stable violation.
pattern.drift_behavior: Outward drift is present but not sufficient to sustain immediate terminal violation.
pattern.slew_behavior: Slew does not establish a unique acceleration signature.
pattern.envelope_relation: Boundary grazing followed by reentry into the admissible envelope.

interpretation.operational_meaning: The trajectory has entered a cautionary boundary regime but does not remain there on the first excursion.

ambiguity_notes: This may reflect an estimator or regime effect as well as genuine degradation structure.
Boundary reentry is not evidence that the underlying degradation cause has disappeared.
exclusion_conditions: Do not call this a healthy trajectory once boundary reentry has occurred.
Do not interpret reentry as mechanism-specific reversal.

supporting_instances.notes: The first boundary event at cycle 17 returns to Admissible at cycle 20 before the later violation.
The first boundary event at cycle 22 returns to Admissible at cycle 25 before the later violation.

counter_examples.notes: The observed boundary regime proceeds directly to violation without a return to Admissible.

entries.transfer_scope: Current NASA PCoE capacity-only traces with explicitly recorded state transitions.
known_failures: The current bank cannot attribute boundary reentry to a unique external regime or measurement condition.

notes: Supporting instances are taken from the current repeated cell evaluation path.

entries.title: Violation recovery followed by repeat structural alarm
entries.regime_scope: NASA PCoE FY08Q4 single-cell capacity-only traces with explicit transition auditing.
admissibility_assumptions: The audit trace explicitly records return transitions from Violation to Admissible.
This entry remains structural and advisory.

pattern.residual_behavior: Residual exits the envelope, returns inside it, and later re-enters Boundary or Violation.
pattern.drift_behavior: Outward drift remains active across a recurrent transition sequence.
pattern.slew_behavior: Slew may vary but does not by itself resolve the ambiguity of the recurrent loop.
pattern.envelope_relation: Violation, reentry into Admissible, and later repeat non-admissible excursion.

interpretation.operational_meaning: The trajectory has already violated the envelope once and later repeats the alarm after reentry.

ambiguity_notes: A recurrent alarm loop does not uniquely identify the cause of the envelope reentry or repeat violation.
This pattern should be treated as structurally unstable rather than mechanistically resolved.
exclusion_conditions: Do not reduce this pattern to a single-cause diagnosis.
Do not infer that one return to Admissible clears the structural concern.

supporting_instances.notes: Violation at cycle 39 is followed by a return to Admissible at 48 and a repeat alarm at cycles 49-50.
Violation at cycle 38 returns to Admissible at 48, then repeats Boundary and Violation at 49-50.
Violation at cycle 29 returns to Admissible at 46 and repeats Boundary and Violation at 47-48.

counter_examples.notes: The observed trajectory does not return to Admissible after violation.

entries.transfer_scope: NASA PCoE capacity-only trajectories with explicit state-transition auditing.
known_failures: The current crate does not model invalid-stream suppression, so recurrent loops are evaluated only from the available capacity structure.

notes: Supporting intervals come from the current per-cell audit traces.

entries.title: Clean monotone escalation
entries.regime_scope: NASA PCoE FY08Q4 single-cell capacity-only traces with no observed return transitions.
admissibility_assumptions: The observed monotone state sequence is specific to the current evaluated trajectory.
The absence of return transitions is an observed property, not a universal rule of the grammar.

pattern.residual_behavior: Residual approaches the boundary and then exits the envelope without later reentry in the observed trace.
pattern.drift_behavior: Outward drift remains negative through the first non-admissible transition sequence.
pattern.slew_behavior: Slew is not required to establish a unique acceleration claim.
pattern.envelope_relation: Boundary approach followed by a short gap to first envelope exit, with no regressive transitions.

interpretation.operational_meaning: The observed trace escalates directly from admissible behavior to violation without returning to a lower grammar state.

ambiguity_notes: This is an observed trajectory motif, not a grammar invariant.
Other cells in the same dataset do show return transitions.
exclusion_conditions: Do not describe the full grammar as monotone-only based on this entry.

supporting_instances.notes: The observed transitions are Admissible -> Boundary -> Violation with no later return to a lower grammar state.

counter_examples.notes: The trace later returns from Violation to Admissible before the final reentry loop.
The trace includes both Boundary -> Admissible and Violation -> Admissible transitions.
The trace includes Boundary and Violation reentry loops.

entries.transfer_scope: Observed only for the current B0007 capacity-only trajectory under the crate's default configuration.
known_failures: This pattern is not representative of all cells even within the current dataset.

notes: Counter-examples are attached from the other evaluated FY08Q4 cells.

entries.title: Early structural lead relative to threshold baseline
entries.regime_scope: Current NASA PCoE single-cell capacity-only runs using the crate's fixed 85% threshold baseline for comparison.
admissibility_assumptions: Lead time is measured against the current threshold baseline definition in the crate.
This entry compares structural timing; it is not a claim of universal superiority.

pattern.residual_behavior: Residual-driven boundary or violation onset appears before the 85% threshold alarm cycle.
pattern.drift_behavior: Outward drift is already present when the structural lead is observed.
pattern.slew_behavior: Slew may or may not be prominent; the lead is defined by timing, not a unique slew signature.
pattern.envelope_relation: Structural boundary/violation onset occurs before threshold crossing.

interpretation.operational_meaning: The DSFB structural onset provides positive cycle lead relative to the current blunt capacity threshold comparator.

ambiguity_notes: Lead depends on the declared 85% threshold baseline used in the crate.
This entry does not establish comparative optimality outside the evaluated workflow.
exclusion_conditions: Do not extrapolate the lead metric to other threshold definitions without rerunning the workflow.

supporting_instances.notes: Boundary onset precedes the threshold baseline by 41 cycles.
Boundary onset precedes the threshold baseline by 29 cycles.
Boundary onset precedes the threshold baseline by 50 cycles.
Boundary onset precedes the threshold baseline by 40 cycles.

entries.transfer_scope: Current NASA PCoE FY08Q4 cell-level repeated evaluation only.
known_failures: The lead metric is comparator-dependent and does not imply general comparative dominance.

notes: Lead is computed from the current cell evaluation helper against the fixed 85% baseline.

entries.title: Terminal persistent structural alarm
entries.regime_scope: Current NASA PCoE FY08Q4 cell-level traces as far as the recorded trajectory end.
admissibility_assumptions: Terminal persistence is defined only relative to the available recorded trajectory end.
This is a trajectory-end observation, not a control action.

pattern.residual_behavior: Residual remains outside the admissibility envelope through the end of the recorded trace after the final violation onset.
pattern.drift_behavior: Outward drift has already established a non-admissible regime before the terminal run.
pattern.slew_behavior: Slew may vary; terminal persistence is defined by the final state run, not a unique slew signature.
pattern.envelope_relation: The final recorded segment remains in Violation.

interpretation.operational_meaning: The observed trajectory ends in a persistent Violation regime and should remain advisory-review relevant.

ambiguity_notes: Terminal persistence is defined only over the recorded dataset horizon.
This does not imply unique physical collapse dynamics.
exclusion_conditions: Do not convert this advisory interpretation into automatic actuation inside the current crate.

supporting_instances.notes: After the final repeat violation at cycle 50, the recorded trajectory ends in Violation.
After the final repeat violation at cycle 50, the recorded trajectory ends in Violation.
The recorded trajectory remains in Violation from the first violation at cycle 38 through the end of the trace.
After the final repeat violation at cycle 48, the recorded trajectory ends in Violation.

entries.transfer_scope: Recorded NASA PCoE trajectory endings only; no claim beyond the available trace horizon.
known_failures: Terminal persistence is truncated by the recorded data horizon.

notes: End-of-trace persistence is taken from the current repeated cell evaluation path.

entries.title: Accelerating fade / knee candidate
entries.regime_scope: Single-channel capacity-only structural rule set with the current drift and slew persistence thresholds.
admissibility_assumptions: Acceleration is defined structurally from persistent drift and persistent slew.
No unique physical mechanism is implied by this entry.

pattern.residual_behavior: Residual remains negative near or beyond the admissibility envelope.
pattern.drift_behavior: Outward drift remains persistently negative beyond the configured threshold.
pattern.slew_behavior: Negative slew remains persistently beyond the configured threshold long enough to trigger the acceleration rule.
pattern.envelope_relation: Boundary or Violation coincides with persistent drift and persistent accelerating slew.

interpretation.operational_meaning: A structural acceleration-style motif is present under the current rule set.

ambiguity_notes: This is not a mechanism-identification claim.
The current default NASA PCoE runs in this crate do not populate this reason code.
exclusion_conditions: Do not promote this entry beyond illustrative status without direct supporting instances.

counter_examples.notes: Current default run records SustainedCapacityFade, not AcceleratingFadeKnee.
Current default run records SustainedCapacityFade, not AcceleratingFadeKnee.
Current default run records SustainedCapacityFade, not AcceleratingFadeKnee.
Current default run records SustainedCapacityFade, not AcceleratingFadeKnee.

entries.transfer_scope: Illustrative only until the current crate produces direct supporting instances.
known_failures: No supporting instances are currently attached under the default NASA PCoE workflow.

notes: This entry remains illustrative because the current default runs do not emit the knee reason code.
